items.title: Deadly MV Hondius Hantavirus Outbreak Is Over, W.H.O. Says
items.title_en: Deadly MV Hondius Hantavirus Outbreak Is Over, W.H.O. Says
items.body: The rodent-borne virus, which sickened passengers on the MV Hondius, killed three people, led to a race to find its origin and a global health alert.
items.body_en: The rodent-borne virus, which sickened passengers on the MV Hondius, killed three people, led to a race to find its origin and a global health alert.

items.title: WHO says Hantavirus outbreak linked to ship is over
items.title_en: WHO says Hantavirus outbreak linked to ship is over
items.body: The World Health Organization's director general says no further cases have been reported since 25 May.
items.body_en: The World Health Organization's director general says no further cases have been reported since 25 May.

items.title: Appel à manifestation d’intérêt : développer des solutions diagnostiques et antivirales contre les hantavirus
items.title_en: Appel à manifestation d’intérêt : développer des solutions diagnostiques et antivirales contre les hantavirus

items.title: L'Argentine détecte un variant d'hantavirus en Terre de Feu
items.title_en: L'Argentine détecte un variant d'hantavirus en Terre de Feu
items.body: Un nouveau variant d'hantavirus a été détecté chez des souris capturées en Terre de Feu, en Argentine. Les autorités sanitaires précisent qu'il est bien distinct de la souche identifiée à bord du navire de croisière Hondius, responsable de trois décès, et poursuivent leurs investigations pour évaluer les risques…
items.body_en: Un nouveau variant d'hantavirus a été détecté chez des souris capturées en Terre de Feu, en Argentine. Les autorités sanitaires précisent qu'il est bien distinct de la souche identifiée à bord du navire de croisière Hondius, responsable de trois décès, et poursuivent leurs investigations pour évaluer les risques…

items.title: Communicable disease threats report, 6 - 12 June 2026, week 24
items.title_en: Communicable disease threats report, 6 - 12 June 2026, week 24
items.body: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 6-12 June, and includes updates on measles, Nipah virus infection, Chikungunya virus disease, Hantavirus, Ebola, and West Nile virus.
items.body_en: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 6-12 June, and includes updates on measles, Nipah virus infection, Chikungunya virus disease, Hantavirus, Ebola, and West Nile virus.

items.title: [Comment] How CEPI can enhance global vaccine equity and transparency
items.title_en: [Comment] How CEPI can enhance global vaccine equity and transparency
items.body: The absence of proven vaccines to address the 2026 outbreaks of hantavirus and Ebola virus disease caused by Bundibugyo virus (species Orthoebolavirus bundibugyoense) underscores the essential role of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI) in protecting everyone's health. On June 1, 2026, CEPI…

On June 1, 2026, CEPI…

items.title: Communicable disease threats report, 23-29 May 2026, week 22
items.title_en: Communicable disease threats report, 23-29 May 2026, week 22
items.body: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 23-29 May 2026, and includes updates on Ebola, hantavirus, cholera, and West Nile virus.
items.body_en: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 23-29 May 2026, and includes updates on Ebola, hantavirus, cholera, and West Nile virus.

items.title: Communicable disease threats report, 14-22 May 2026, week 21
items.title_en: Communicable disease threats report, 14-22 May 2026, week 21
items.body: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 14-22 May 2026, and includes updates on hantavirus, chikungunya, Ebola disease outbreak, avian influenza, measles, and an overview of respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA.
items.body_en: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 14-22 May 2026, and includes updates on hantavirus, chikungunya, Ebola disease outbreak, avian influenza, measles, and an overview of respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA.

items.title: Follow-up message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response
items.title_en: Follow-up message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response
items.body: A message from the Director-General of the World Health Organization expressing gratitude to the people of Tenerife for their solidarity and successful cooperation during the operation to safely disembark and care for over 120 passengers from 23 countries amid an outbreak of hantavirus.
items.body_en: A message from the Director-General of the World Health Organization expressing gratitude to the people of Tenerife for their solidarity and successful cooperation during the operation to safely disembark and care for over 120 passengers from 23 countries amid an outbreak of hantavirus.

items.title: Communicable disease threats report, 9-13 May 2026, week 20
items.title_en: Communicable disease threats report, 9-13 May 2026, week 20
items.body: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 9-13 May 2026, and includes updates on avian influenza, human cases of swine influenza, mpox, and hantavirus.
items.body_en: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 9-13 May 2026, and includes updates on avian influenza, human cases of swine influenza, mpox, and hantavirus.

items.title: Communicable disease threats report, 2-8 May 2026, week 19
items.title_en: Communicable disease threats report, 2-8 May 2026, week 19
items.body: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 2-8 May 2026, and includes updates on hantavirus, mpox, Middle East respiratory syndrome coronavirus (MERS-CoV), Salmonella Stanley, and an overview of respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA.
items.body_en: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 2-8 May 2026, and includes updates on hantavirus, mpox, Middle East respiratory syndrome coronavirus (MERS-CoV), Salmonella Stanley, and an overview of respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA.
